zh.Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, United: 美國新罕布夏州斯特拉福德多福 姆姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Heard於1620年出生在英國爾迪維頓時，她的父親James Luke Heard21歲，母親Sarah Conley22歲。她於1638年與James S. Nute在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾斯特拉福德多福結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1671年在美國新罕布夏州斯特拉福德多福 姆姆享年51歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Heard于1620年出生在英国爾迪維頓时，她的父亲James Luke Heard21岁，母亲Sarah Conley22岁。她于1638年与James S. Nute在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾斯特拉福德多福结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1671年在美国新罕布什尔州斯特拉福德多福 姆姆享年51岁。

nameMeanings.name: Heard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English herd(e), hird(e), hord(e), Older Scots herd, hird ‘herdsman’, i.e. one who looked after cattle, sheep, or goats (Old English heorde, hierde). Compare Hardman .

variants.name: Hirt
meanings: German: occupational name for a herdsman, Middle High German hirt, cognate with English Heard .

variants.name: Hardman
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English herdeman, herdema+n, hurdeman (Old English hierdeman, heordman) ‘herdsman, one who tends livestock’. Compare Heard .
English: occasionally from the Middle English personal name Hardman (Old English Heardmann, literally ‘hard man’).
English and Irish: shortened form of   Hardeman  .

variants.name: Heird
meanings: Scottish: probably a variant of   Heard  .

variants.name: Hard
meanings: English: variant of   Heard  . Compare   Hardman  .

English, German, Dutch, and Swedish (Hård): nickname for a stern or severe man, from Middle English, Middle Low German hard, Middle Dutch hart, hert, Swedish hård ‘hard, inflexible’. The Swedish name was probably originally a soldier's name.

English: topographic name denoting a dweller on hard, firm ground, from a transferred use of the adjective in 2 above, or perhaps sometimes from a landing-place, as in the modern Hampshire use of the term hard. Compare Hardacre .

variants.name: Herd
meanings: Scottish (Fife) and English: variant of   Heard  .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Heard was born in 1620, in Tiverton, Cheshire, England, her father, James Luke Heard, was 21 and her mother, Sarah Conley, was 22. She married James S. Nute in 1638, in Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1671, in Dover MM, Strafford, New Hampshire, United States, at the age of 51.

Nute en 1638, à Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Heardが1620年にイギリスチェシュアイアティバートンで生まれた時，父James Luke Heardは21歳，母Sarah Conleyは22歳であった。1638年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアストラフォードドバーでJames S. Nuteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1671年にアメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州ストラフォードドバー ンムで51歳で死去した。

ko.Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, British: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 스트래퍼드 도버
ko.Dover, Strafford, New Hampshire, Colonia: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 스트래퍼드 도버
ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Heard(이)가 1620년 영국 체실르 티버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James Luke Heard(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah Conley(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1638년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 스트래퍼드 도버에서 James S. Nute(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1671년 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 스트래퍼드 도버에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Heard родилась в 1620 г. в местности тивиртон, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени James Luke Heard было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Sarah Conley, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James S. Nute в 1638 г. в местности довир, страффорд, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1671 г. в местности довир мм, страффорд, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 51 год.
